text:
Market today: Risk-on mood driven by AWS dropping $1B into a new AI unit - that's a direct shot of confidence into cloud/AI names, which is the engine of your entire book. No macro shocks today; Supreme Court rulings and yen weakness are background noise for tech.

Your holdings: PTNM is your only real concern - down 15.25% and bumping against its $9.50 stop. On margin, a spec that's already bleeding this much needs a hard look. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; margin amplifies any sudden drop fast. SOXL is 3x leveraged - it's up 2.48% but the gap between current price ($260) and stop ($200) is huge; a bad day hits three times harder.

Watch: CRWD scanner is flagging a clean re-entry zone ($740-$744) if it pulls back - you're already long and green. AWS news keeps cloud/AI names bid.

Bottom line: Protect PTNM at the stop and keep SOXL/SPCX sized for the pain they can cause on margin.
plays: PTNM: Already -15.25% and approaching your $9.50 stop - if it breaks, don't hesitate. On margin, this spec has earned its exit.
CRWD: You're already long (+6.22%). Scanner shows clean re-entry $740-$744, stop $731. If it pulls back there, consider adding a small layer, not a full new position.
SOXL: No new add today - you're in and green, but $260 with a $200 stop on 3x leverage is a wide gap. No reason to add exposure here without a tighter level.
